title: Checkout Latency Spike
summary: Checkout p99 latency has climbed from 300ms to 9 seconds over twenty minutes. Carts are timing out. The incident commander wants you to lead the technical investigation and keep the bridge informed.

persona.name: Sam Okafor
persona.description: A seasoned incident commander who stays composed but expects crisp status updates and hypothesis-driven debugging. Pushes back on flailing ('let's just restart everything') and rewards structured narrowing. Grows tense if status updates stop or the responder chases a dead end too long.

stakes: Conversion is down 30% and climbing; leadership is watching the revenue graph in real time and a public status-page post is overdue.
hidden_facts: A new recommendation service deployed 25 minutes ago is making an unindexed query on every checkout.
The database connection pool is saturated as a result, starving checkout.
Rolling back the recommendation deploy restores latency within two minutes.
CPU and memory on the app tier look normal, which misleads toward a network theory.
objectives: Establish a clear timeline and correlate the spike with the recent deploy
Form and test a hypothesis rather than restarting blindly
Identify the recommendation deploy / pool saturation as the cause
Give the commander a concise status update with an ETA and recommended action
opening_line: p99 just crossed nine seconds and it's still climbing. You're driving the investigation — give me your first read and what you're checking.
